text: On Oct. 30, 1962, the U.S. conducted the largest nuclear bomb explosion in history, on the atoll of Namu in what is now the Marshall Islands. Testing a 15-megaton hydrogen bomb left the Marshall Islands awash in radiation and marked the start of a program of research into the health damage caused by radiation from nuclear weapons. The Marshall Islanders had their human rights violated in the name of this research. It is now believed that radioactive fallout from Pacific tests also drifted to other islands in Micronesia. The US must release all classified information it has concerning the cold war nuclear tests, the air drift of the fallout and the effects of ionizing radiation on the populations of Micronesia. We urge the international health community to call on nuclear powers to act on their obligations under the Treaty on the Non-Proliferation of Nuclear Weapons with the objective of achieving the total elimination of nuclear weapons. We urge the US government to act on justice and basic human rights principles by expanding Medicaid to cover all citizens of Compact of Free Association nations.
